rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: The Court does not believe that the Motion to Enforce Settlement was a novel, complex motion that required the services of three partners and two associates. The motion involved the interpretation of the terms of a settlement agreement, and whether those terms barred the petition in question. That is a common issue that arises in a variety of scenarios. On the other hand, if Petitioner contends that the hourly rates charged by Respondent's attorneys are unreasonable, the Court would request that Petitioner provide the Court with the hourly rates charged by its attorney on this motion. Similarly, if Petitioner contends that the number of hours expended on the motion by Respondent's attorneys is unreasonable, the Court would request that Petitioner provide the Court with the number of hours charged by its attorneys to this motion. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, June 21, 2017.
